verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचऐलस्य चोर्वशीगर्भात् षडासन्नात्मजा नृप ।आयु: श्रुतायु: सत्यायू रयोऽथ विजयो जय: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācaailasya corvaśī-garbhātṣaḍ āsann ātmajā nṛpaāyuḥ śrutāyuḥ satyāyūrayo ’tha vijayo jayaḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī continued: O King Parīkṣit, from the womb of Urvaśī, six sons were generated by Purūravā. Their names were Āyu, Śrutāyu, Satyāyu, Raya, Vijaya and Jaya.

verses.sanskrit: श्रुतायोर्वसुमान् पुत्र: सत्यायोश्च श्रुतञ्जय: ।रयस्य सुत एकश्च जयस्य तनयोऽमित: ॥ २ ॥भीमस्तु विजयस्याथ काञ्चनो होत्रकस्तत: ।तस्य जह्नु: सुतो गङ्गां गण्डूषीकृत्य योऽपिबत् ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrutāyor vasumān putraḥsatyāyoś ca śrutañjayaḥrayasya suta ekaś cajayasya tanayo ’mitaḥ
verses.text: The son of Śrutāyu was Vasumān; the son of Satyāyu, Śrutañjaya; the son of Raya, Eka; the son of Jaya, Amita; and the son of Vijaya, Bhīma. The son of Bhīma was Kāñcana; the son of Kāñcana was Hotraka; and the son of Hotraka was Jahnu, who drank all the water of the Ganges in one sip.

verses.sanskrit: जह्नोस्तु पुरुस्तस्याथ बलाकश्चात्मजोऽजक: ।तत: कुश: कुशस्यापि कुशाम्बुस्तनयो वसु: ।कुशनाभश्च चत्वारो गाधिरासीत् कुशाम्बुज: ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: jahnos tu purus tasyāthabalākaś cātmajo ’jakaḥtataḥ kuśaḥ kuśasyāpikuśāmbus tanayo vasuḥkuśanābhaś ca catvārogādhir āsīt kuśāmbujaḥ
verses.text: The son of Jahnu was Puru, the son of Puru was Balāka, the son of Balāka was Ajaka, and the son of Ajaka was Kuśa. Kuśa had four sons, named Kuśāmbu, Tanaya, Vasu and Kuśanābha. The son of Kuśāmbu was Gādhi.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य सत्यवतीं कन्यामृचीकोऽयाचत द्विज: ।वरं विसद‍ृशं मत्वा गाधिर्भार्गवमब्रवीत् ॥ ५ ॥एकत: श्यामकर्णानां हयानां चन्द्रवर्चसाम् ।सहस्रं दीयतां शुल्कं कन्याया: कुशिका वयम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasya satyavatīṁ kanyāmṛcīko ’yācata dvijaḥvaraṁ visadṛśaṁ matvāgādhir bhārgavam abravīt
verses.text: King Gādhi had a daughter named Satyavatī, whom a brāhmaṇa sage named Ṛcīka requested from the King to be his wife. King Gādhi, however, regarded Ṛcīka as an unfit husband for his daughter, and therefore he told the brāhmaṇa, “My dear sir, I belong to the dynasty of Kuśa. Because we are aristocratic kṣatriyas, you have to give some dowry for my daughter. Therefore, bring at least one thousand horses, each as brilliant as moonshine and each having one black ear, whether right or left.”

verses.sanskrit: इत्युक्तस्तन्मतं ज्ञात्वा गत: स वरुणान्तिकम् ।आनीय दत्त्वा तानश्वानुपयेमे वराननाम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity uktas tan-mataṁ jñātvāgataḥ sa varuṇāntikamānīya dattvā tān aśvānupayeme varānanām
verses.text: When King Gādhi made this demand, the great sage Ṛcīka could understand the King’s mind. Therefore he went to the demigod Varuṇa and brought from him the one thousand horses that Gādhi had demanded. After delivering these horses, the sage married the King’s beautiful daughter.

verses.sanskrit: स ऋषि: प्रार्थित: पत्‍न्या श्वश्र्वा चापत्यकाम्यया ।श्रपयित्वोभयैर्मन्त्रैश्चरुं स्‍नातुं गतो मुनि: ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ṛṣiḥ prārthitaḥ patnyāśvaśrvā cāpatya-kāmyayāśrapayitvobhayair mantraiścaruṁ snātuṁ gato muniḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, Ṛcīka Muni’s wife and mother-in-law, each desiring a son, requested the Muni to prepare an oblation. Thus Ṛcīka Muni prepared one oblation for his wife with a brāhmaṇa mantra and another for his mother-in-law with a kṣatriya mantra. Then he went out to bathe.

verses.sanskrit: तावत् सत्यवती मात्रा स्वचरुं याचिता सती ।श्रेष्ठं मत्वा तयायच्छन्मात्रे मातुरदत् स्वयम् ॥ ९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāvat satyavatī mātrāsva-caruṁ yācitā satīśreṣṭhaṁ matvā tayāyacchanmātre mātur adat svayam
verses.text: Meanwhile, because Satyavatī’s mother thought that the oblation prepared for her daughter, Ṛcīka’s wife, must be better, she asked her daughter for that oblation. Satyavatī therefore gave her own oblation to her mother and ate her mother’s oblation herself.

verses.sanskrit: तद् विदित्वा मुनि: प्राह पत्नीं कष्टमकारषी: ।घोरो दण्डधर: पुत्रो भ्राता ते ब्रह्मवित्तम: ॥ १० ॥
verses.transliteration: tad viditvā muniḥ prāhapatnīṁ kaṣṭam akāraṣīḥghoro daṇḍa-dharaḥ putrobhrātā te brahma-vittamaḥ
verses.text: When the great sage Ṛcīka returned home after bathing and understood what had happened in his absence, he said to his wife, Satyavatī, “You have done a great wrong. Your son will be a fierce kṣatriya, able to punish everyone, and your brother will be a learned scholar in spiritual science.”

verses.sanskrit: प्रसादित: सत्यवत्या मैवं भूरिति भार्गव: ।अथ तर्हि भवेत् पौत्रो जमदग्निस्ततोऽभवत् ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: prasāditaḥ satyavatyāmaivaṁ bhūr iti bhārgavaḥatha tarhi bhavet pautrojamadagnis tato ’bhavat
verses.text: Satyavatī, however, pacified Ṛcīka Muni with peaceful words and requested that her son not be like a fierce kṣatriya. Ṛcīka Muni replied, “Then your grandson will be of a kṣatriya spirit.” Thus Jamadagni was born as the son of Satyavatī.

verses.sanskrit: सा चाभूत् सुमहत्पुण्या कौशिकी लोकपावनी ।रेणो: सुतां रेणुकां वै जमदग्निरुवाह याम् ॥ १२ ॥तस्यां वै भार्गवऋषे: सुता वसुमदादय: ।यवीयाञ्जज्ञ एतेषां राम इत्यभिविश्रुत: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sā cābhūt sumahat-puṇyākauśikī loka-pāvanīreṇoḥ sutāṁ reṇukāṁ vaijamadagnir uvāha yām
verses.text: Satyavatī later became the sacred river Kauśikī to purify the entire world, and her son, Jamadagni, married Reṇukā, the daughter of Reṇu. By the semen of Jamadagni, many sons, headed by Vasumān, were born from the womb of Reṇukā. The youngest of them was named Rāma, or Paraśurāma.

verses.sanskrit: यमाहुर्वासुदेवांशं हैहयानां कुलान्तकम् ।त्रि:सप्तकृत्वो य इमां चक्रे नि:क्षत्रियां महीम् ॥ १४ ॥
verses.transliteration: yam āhur vāsudevāṁśaṁhaihayānāṁ kulāntakamtriḥ-sapta-kṛtvo ya imāṁcakre niḥkṣatriyāṁ mahīm
verses.text: Learned scholars accept this Paraśurāma as the celebrated incarnation of Vāsudeva who annihilated the dynasty of Kārtavīrya. Paraśurāma killed all the kṣatriyas on earth twenty-one times.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृप्तं क्षत्रं भुवो भारमब्रह्मण्यमनीनशत् ।रजस्तमोवृतमहन् फल्गुन्यपि कृतेꣷहसि ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛptaṁ kṣatraṁ bhuvo bhāramabrahmaṇyam anīnaśatrajas-tamo-vṛtam ahanphalguny api kṛte ’ṁhasi
verses.text: When the royal dynasty, being excessively proud because of the material modes of passion and ignorance, became irreligious and ceased to care for the laws enacted by the brāhmaṇas, Paraśurāma killed them. Although their offense was not very severe, he killed them to lessen the burden of the world.

verses.sanskrit: श्रीराजोवाचकिं तदंहो भगवतो राजन्यैरजितात्मभि: ।कृतं येन कुलं नष्टं क्षत्रियाणामभीक्ष्णश: ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-rājovācakiṁ tad aṁho bhagavatorājanyair ajitātmabhiḥkṛtaṁ yena kulaṁ naṣṭaṁkṣatriyāṇām abhīkṣṇaśaḥ
verses.text: King Parīkṣit inquired from Śukadeva Gosvāmī: What was the offense that the kṣatriyas who could not control their senses committed before Lord Paraśurāma, the incarnation of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, for which the Lord annihilated the kṣatriya dynasty again and again?

verses.sanskrit: श्रीबादरायणिरुवाचहैहयानामधिपतिरर्जुन: क्षत्रियर्षभ: ।दत्तं नारायणांशांशमाराध्य परिकर्मभि: ॥ १७ ॥बाहून् दशशतं लेभे दुर्धर्षत्वमरातिषु ।अव्याहतेन्द्रियौज:श्रीतेजोवीर्ययशोबलम् ॥ १८ ॥योगेश्वरत्वमैश्वर्यं गुणा यत्राणिमादय: ।चचाराव्याहतगतिर्लोकेषु पवनो यथा ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-bādarāyaṇir uvācahaihayānām adhipatirarjunaḥ kṣatriyarṣabhaḥdattaṁ nārāyaṇāṁśāṁśamārādhya parikarmabhiḥ
verses.text: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: The best of the kṣatriyas, Kārtavīryārjuna, the King of the Haihayas, received one thousand arms by worshiping Dattātreya, the plenary expansion of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Nārāyaṇa. He also became undefeatable by enemies and received unobstructed sensory power, beauty, influence, strength, fame and the mystic power by which to achieve all the perfections of yoga, such as aṇimā and laghimā. Thus having become fully opulent, he roamed all over the universe without opposition, just like the wind.

verses.sanskrit: स्त्रीरत्नैरावृत: क्रीडन् रेवाम्भसि मदोत्कट: ।वैजयन्तीं स्रजं बिभ्रद् रुरोध सरितं भुजै: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: strī-ratnair āvṛtaḥ krīḍanrevāmbhasi madotkaṭaḥvaijayantīṁ srajaṁ bibhradrurodha saritaṁ bhujaiḥ
verses.text: Once while enjoying in the water of the river Narmadā, the puffed-up Kārtavīryārjuna, surrounded by beautiful women and garlanded with a garland of victory, stopped the flow of the water with his arms.

verses.sanskrit: विप्लावितं स्वशिबिरं प्रतिस्रोत:सरिज्जलै: ।नामृष्यत् तस्य तद् वीर्यं वीरमानी दशानन: ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: viplāvitaṁ sva-śibiraṁpratisrotaḥ-sarij-jalaiḥnāmṛṣyat tasya tad vīryaṁvīramānī daśānanaḥ
verses.text: Because Kārtavīryārjuna made the water flow in the opposite direction, the camp of Rāvaṇa, which was set up on the bank of the Narmadā near the city of Māhiṣmatī, was inundated. This was unbearable to the ten-headed Rāvaṇa, who considered himself a great hero and could not tolerate Kārtavīryārjuna’s power.

verses.sanskrit: गृहीतो लीलया स्त्रीणां समक्षं कृतकिल्बिष: ।माहिष्मत्यां सन्निरुद्धो मुक्तो येन कपिर्यथा ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: gṛhīto līlayā strīṇāṁsamakṣaṁ kṛta-kilbiṣaḥmāhiṣmatyāṁ sanniruddhomukto yena kapir yathā
verses.text: When Rāvaṇa attempted to insult Kārtavīryārjuna in the presence of the women and thus offended him, Kārtavīryārjuna easily arrested Rāvaṇa and put him in custody in the city of Māhiṣmatī, just as one captures a monkey, and then released him neglectfully.

verses.sanskrit: स एकदा तु मृगयां विचरन् विजने वने ।यद‍ृच्छयाश्रमपदं जमदग्नेरुपाविशत् ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa ekadā tu mṛgayāṁvicaran vijane vaneyadṛcchayāśrama-padaṁjamadagner upāviśat
verses.text: Once while Kārtavīryārjuna was wandering unengaged in a solitary forest and hunting, he approached the residence of Jamadagni.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मै स नरदेवाय मुनिरर्हणमाहरत् ।ससैन्यामात्यवाहाय हविष्मत्या तपोधन: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: tasmai sa naradevāyamunir arhaṇam āharatsasainyāmātya-vāhāyahaviṣmatyā tapo-dhanaḥ
verses.text: The sage Jamadagni, who was engaged in great austerities in the forest, received the King very well, along with the King’s soldiers, ministers and carriers. He supplied all the necessities to worship these guests, for he possessed a kāmadhenu cow that was able to supply everything.

verses.sanskrit: स वै रत्नं तु तद् दृष्ट्वा आत्मैश्वर्यातिशायनम् ।तन्नाद्रियताग्निहोत्र्यां साभिलाष: सहैहय: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa vai ratnaṁ tu tad dṛṣṭvāātmaiśvaryātiśāyanamtan nādriyatāgnihotryāṁsābhilāṣaḥ sahaihayaḥ
verses.text: Kārtavīryārjuna thought that Jamadagni was more powerful and wealthy than himself because of possessing a jewel in the form of the kāmadhenu. Therefore he and his own men, the Haihayas, were not very much appreciative of Jamadagni’s reception. On the contrary, they wanted to possess that kāmadhenu, which was useful for the execution of the agnihotra sacrifice.

verses.sanskrit: हविर्धानीमृषेर्दर्पान्नरान् हर्तुमचोदयत् ।ते च माहिष्मतीं निन्यु: सवत्सां क्रन्दतीं बलात् ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: havirdhānīm ṛṣer darpānnarān hartum acodayatte ca māhiṣmatīṁ ninyuḥsa-vatsāṁ krandatīṁ balāt
verses.text: Being puffed up by material power, Kārtavīryārjuna encouraged his men to steal Jamadagni’s kāmadhenu. Thus the men forcibly took away the crying kāmadhenu, along with her calf, to Māhiṣmatī, Kārtavīryārjuna’s capital.

verses.sanskrit: अथ राजनि निर्याते राम आश्रम आगत: ।श्रुत्वा तत् तस्य दौरात्म्यं चुक्रोधाहिरिवाहत: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: atha rājani niryāterāma āśrama āgataḥśrutvā tat tasya daurātmyaṁcukrodhāhir ivāhataḥ
verses.text: Thereafter, Kārtavīryārjuna having left with the kāmadhenu, Paraśurāma returned to the āśrama. When Paraśurāma, the youngest son of Jamadagni, heard about Kārtavīryārjuna’s nefarious deed, he became as angry as a trampled snake.

verses.sanskrit: घोरमादाय परशुं सतूणं वर्म कार्मुकम् ।अन्वधावत दुर्मर्षो मृगेन्द्र इव यूथपम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: ghoram ādāya paraśuṁsatūṇaṁ varma kārmukamanvadhāvata durmarṣomṛgendra iva yūthapam
verses.text: Taking up his fierce chopper, his shield, his bow and a quiver of arrows, Lord Paraśurāma, exceedingly angry, chased Kārtavīryārjuna just as a lion chases an elephant.

verses.sanskrit: तमापतन्तं भृगुवर्यमोजसाधनुर्धरं बाणपरश्वधायुधम् ।ऐणेयचर्माम्बरमर्कधामभि-र्युतं जटाभिर्दद‍ृशे पुरीं विशन् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: tam āpatantaṁ bhṛgu-varyam ojasādhanur-dharaṁ bāṇa-paraśvadhāyudhamaiṇeya-carmāmbaram arka-dhāmabhiryutaṁ jaṭābhir dadṛśe purīṁ viśan
verses.text: As King Kārtavīryārjuna entered his capital, Māhiṣmatī Purī, he saw Lord Paraśurāma, the best of the Bhṛgu dynasty, coming after him, holding a chopper, shield, bow and arrows. Lord Paraśurāma was covered with a black deerskin, and his matted locks of hair appeared like the sunshine.

verses.sanskrit: अचोदयद्धस्तिरथाश्वपत्तिभि-र्गदासिबाणर्ष्टिशतघ्निशक्तिभि: ।अक्षौहिणी: सप्तदशातिभीषणा-स्ता राम एको भगवानसूदयत् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: acodayad dhasti-rathāśva-pattibhirgadāsi-bāṇarṣṭi-śataghni-śaktibhiḥakṣauhiṇīḥ sapta-daśātibhīṣaṇāstā rāma eko bhagavān asūdayat
verses.text: Upon seeing Paraśurāma, Kārtavīryārjuna immediately feared him and sent many elephants, chariots, horses and infantry soldiers equipped with clubs, swords, arrows, ṛṣṭis, śataghnis, śaktis, and many similar weapons to fight against him. Kārtavīryārjuna sent seventeen full akṣauhiṇīs of soldiers to check Paraśurāma. But Lord Paraśurāma alone killed all of them.

verses.sanskrit: यतो यतोऽसौ प्रहरत्परश्वधोमनोऽनिलौजा: परचक्रसूदन: ।ततस्ततश्छिन्नभुजोरुकन्धरानिपेतुरुर्व्यां हतसूतवाहना: ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: yato yato ’sau praharat-paraśvadhomano-’nilaujāḥ para-cakra-sūdanaḥtatas tataś chinna-bhujoru-kandharānipetur urvyāṁ hata-sūta-vāhanāḥ
verses.text: Lord Paraśurāma, being expert in killing the military strength of the enemy, worked with the speed of the mind and the wind, slicing his enemies with his chopper [paraśu]. Wherever he went, the enemies fell, their legs, arms and shoulders being severed, their chariot drivers killed, and their carriers, the elephants and horses, all annihilated.

verses.sanskrit: द‍ृष्ट्वा स्वसैन्यं रुधिरौघकर्दमेरणाजिरे रामकुठारसायकै: ।विवृक्णवर्मध्वजचापविग्रहंनिपातितं हैहय आपतद् रुषा ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: dṛṣṭvā sva-sainyaṁ rudhiraugha-kardameraṇājire rāma-kuṭhāra-sāyakaiḥvivṛkṇa-varma-dhvaja-cāpa-vigrahaṁnipātitaṁ haihaya āpatad ruṣā
verses.text: By manipulating his axe and arrows, Lord Paraśurāma cut to pieces the shields, flags, bows and bodies of Kārtavīryārjuna’s soldiers, who fell on the battlefield, muddying the ground with their blood. Seeing these reverses, Kārtavīryārjuna, infuriated, rushed to the battlefield.

verses.sanskrit: अथार्जुन: पञ्चशतेषु बाहुभि-र्धनु:षु बाणान् युगपत् स सन्दधे ।रामाय रामोऽस्त्रभृतां समग्रणी-स्तान्येकधन्वेषुभिराच्छिनत् समम् ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: athārjunaḥ pañca-śateṣu bāhubhirdhanuḥṣu bāṇān yugapat sa sandadherāmāya rāmo ’stra-bhṛtāṁ samagraṇīstāny eka-dhanveṣubhir ācchinat samam
verses.text: Then Kārtavīryārjuna, with his one thousand arms, simultaneously fixed arrows on five hundred bows to kill Lord Paraśurāma. But Lord Paraśurāma, the best of fighters, released enough arrows with only one bow to cut to pieces immediately all the arrows and bows in the hands of Kārtavīryārjuna.

verses.sanskrit: पुन: स्वहस्तैरचलान् मृधेऽङ्‌घ्रिपा-नुत्क्षिप्य वेगादभिधावतो युधि ।भुजान् कुठारेण कठोरनेमिनाचिच्छेद राम: प्रसभं त्वहेरिव ॥ ३४ ॥
verses.transliteration: punaḥ sva-hastair acalān mṛdhe ’ṅghripānutkṣipya vegād abhidhāvato yudhibhujān kuṭhāreṇa kaṭhora-nemināciccheda rāmaḥ prasabhaṁ tv aher iva
verses.text: When his arrows were cut to pieces, Kārtavīryārjuna uprooted many trees and hills with his own hands and again rushed strongly toward Lord Paraśurāma to kill him. But Paraśurāma then used his axe with great force to cut off Kārtavīryārjuna’s arms, just as one might lop off the hoods of a serpent.

verses.sanskrit: कृत्तबाहो: शिरस्तस्य गिरे: श‍ृङ्गमिवाहरत् ।हते पितरि तत्पुत्रा अयुतं दुद्रुवुर्भयात् ॥ ३५ ॥अग्निहोत्रीमुपावर्त्य सवत्सां परवीरहा ।समुपेत्याश्रमं पित्रे परिक्लिष्टां समर्पयत् ॥ ३६ ॥
verses.transliteration: kṛtta-bāhoḥ śiras tasyagireḥ śṛṅgam ivāharathate pitari tat-putrāayutaṁ dudruvur bhayāt
verses.text: Thereafter, Paraśurāma cut off like a mountain peak the head of Kārtavīryārjuna, who had already lost his arms. When Kārtavīryārjuna’s ten thousand sons saw their father killed, they all fled in fear. Then Paraśurāma, having killed the enemy, released the kāmadhenu, which had undergone great suffering, and brought it back with its calf to his residence, where he gave it to his father, Jamadagni.

verses.sanskrit: स्वकर्म तत्कृतं राम: पित्रे भ्रातृभ्य एव च ।वर्णयामास तच्छ्रुत्वा जमदग्निरभाषत ॥ ३७ ॥
verses.transliteration: sva-karma tat kṛtaṁ rāmaḥpitre bhrātṛbhya eva cavarṇayām āsa tac chrutvājamadagnir abhāṣata
verses.text: Paraśurāma described to his father and brothers his activities in killing Kārtavīryārjuna. Upon hearing of these deeds, Jamadagni spoke to his son as follows.

verses.sanskrit: राम राम महाबाहो भवान् पापमकारषीत् ।अवधीन्नरदेवं यत्सर्वदेवमयं वृथा ॥ ३८ ॥
verses.transliteration: rāma rāma mahābāhobhavān pāpam akāraṣītavadhīn naradevaṁ yatsarva-devamayaṁ vṛthā
verses.text: O great hero, my dear son Paraśurāma, you have unnecessarily killed the king, who is supposed to be the embodiment of all the demigods. Thus you have committed a sin.

verses.sanskrit: वयं हि ब्राह्मणास्तात क्षमयार्हणतां गता: ।यया लोकगुरुर्देव: पारमेष्ठ्यमगात् पदम् ॥ ३९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vayaṁ hi brāhmaṇās tātakṣamayārhaṇatāṁ gatāḥyayā loka-gurur devaḥpārameṣṭhyam agāt padam
verses.text: My dear son, we are all brāhmaṇas and have become worshipable for the people in general because of our quality of forgiveness. It is because of this quality that Lord Brahmā, the supreme spiritual master of this universe, has achieved his post.

verses.sanskrit: क्षमया रोचते लक्ष्मीर्ब्राह्मी सौरी यथा प्रभा ।क्षमिणामाशु भगवांस्तुष्यते हरिरीश्वर: ॥ ४० ॥
verses.transliteration: kṣamayā rocate lakṣmīrbrāhmī saurī yathā prabhākṣamiṇām āśu bhagavāṁstuṣyate harir īśvaraḥ
verses.text: The duty of a brāhmaṇa is to culture the quality of forgiveness, which is illuminating like the sun. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Hari, is pleased with those who are forgiving.

verses.sanskrit: राज्ञो मूर्धाभिषिक्तस्य वधो ब्रह्मवधाद् गुरु: ।तीर्थसंसेवया चांहो जह्यङ्गाच्युतचेतन: ॥ ४१ ॥
verses.transliteration: rājño mūrdhābhiṣiktasyavadho brahma-vadhād guruḥtīrtha-saṁsevayā cāṁhojahy aṅgācyuta-cetanaḥ
verses.text: My dear son, killing a king who is an emperor is more severely sinful than killing a brāhmaṇa. But now, if you become Kṛṣṇa conscious and worship the holy places, you can atone for this great sin.
